privileged_info_summary: The colorize task is a GIMP image editing task where an agent applies sepia tone colorization to a color photograph. The task uses real Unsplash images (800x600 JPEG format) as input. Sepia colorization is verified by analyzing the output image in HSV color space: checking hue uniformity (standard deviation < 20°), target hue matching (median hue within 30 ± 20°, i.e., 10-50°), saturation presence (mean > 0.2 on 0-1 scale), and luminosity preservation (brightness correlation > 0.85). The task succeeds when at least 3 of 4 verification criteria are met (75% score threshold). The colorization effect should convert the input image's diverse color palette to monochromatic warm brown/orange tones characteristic of sepia photography. No synthetic data is used; both primary and alternative image URLs are accessible and serve real photographs from Unsplash.
